goal: Separate terrain, walls, ceiling, props, decals, and lights into modular descriptor/render ownership so content kits can compose richer scenes without hardcoded one-offs.

rootIdeas.hypothesis: Separating wall geometry/material descriptors from floor terrain descriptors will make brick, panel, damage, grime, and collision ownership easier to evolve.
rootIdeas.whyItMatters: The current corridor has floor/wall separation, but wall surfaces are still visually monolithic. A separate wall domain lets the renderer apply brick/panel kits while floor keeps damp terrain/grime kits.

subtests.test: Emit explicit descriptors for wall, floor, ceiling, trim, decal, prop, and light surfaces.
acceptance: Render layer can choose materials by surface domain without checking raw maze cell values everywhere.
Debug extract reports counts per surface domain.
No change to movement or collision.

subtests.test: Add a generic wall-surface kit with brick/block and panel profile presets.
acceptance: HorrorCorridor-specific brick colors live in preset/config.
Generic kit does not import game constants.
Wall pattern is visible in player screenshot.

subtests.test: Add separate floor terrain material profiles for damp concrete, slime residue, and worn route marks.
acceptance: Floor projection and wall projection can be tuned independently.
Ooze/trail decals remain readable on top.

risks: Over-separating geometry can increase draw calls if instancing is not preserved.
Material domains must remain descriptor-driven, not hardcoded into gameplay state.

rootIdeas.hypothesis: Longer play requires cumulative content layers: route landmarks, anomaly states, prop clusters, traversal pressure, and recovery cues.

subtests.test: Generate recognizably different corridor regions with tables, crates, pipes, panels, cables, stains, and lights.
acceptance: Players can describe where they are without using minimap only.
At least one midground landmark appears in standard validation screenshots.

subtests.test: Change dressing density and lighting as players approach objectives.
acceptance: Anomaly zones feel different from normal corridors.
Objective direction is implied by world content, not HUD text.

risks: More content can reduce readability if lighting balance is not kept as the acceptance gate.

work: Keep existing instanced world render path stable.
Route wall/floor/ceiling materials through separate descriptor profiles.
blockedBy: Need one current screenshot baseline before changing material domains.
